title: Labor Force Indicators by Area/Sex: Participation, Unemployment, Sector Shares (SH1380-1399)
notes: Direct SCI Yearbook 1399 source (Table 4.2). Conceptually related to the existing iran_disasters__labor_force_participation_rate_urban_rural_2005_2014 chart (itself an extends-row on wdi__SL.TLF.CACT, 2005-2014, iran-data-portal-sourced) but kept as its own new entry rather than a 2-hop extends chain: wider year range (2001-2020) and adds youth unemployment (15-24, 15-29), underemployment share, and employment-share-by-sector columns not in that chart. One flagged anomaly (male/1399 underemployment printed as '4.10' in source, visually confirmed not an extraction error).

title: Total Country Budget: Resources & Uses Breakdown (SH1385-1400)
notes: Extends, in concept, the majlis budget-law series (chart_id majlis__national_budget_totals_by_fiscal_year, itself an extends-row on wdi__GC.XPN.TOTL, FY1341-1370/1963-1992) forward by over a decade to FY1400 (2021), via a different primary source (SCI Yearbook Table 20.1, not the Majlis law texts themselves) -- kept as its own new entry rather than a 2-hop extends chain. 1400 figures are approved/projected, not actual, flagged per-row in the underlying CSV.

title: Consumer Price Index by COICOP Group incl. Durable/Semi-durable/Non-durable Split (1395=100, SH1390-1399)
notes: SCI's own official CPI broken into ~35 major/minor/special groups, including a Durable/Semi-durable/Non-durable goods split (Durable goods +81.6% in 1399 alone) and a 'Household furnishings and appliances' line -- directly the appliance/durable-goods inflation angle this project tracks; not available at this granularity from WDI's single aggregate CPI indicator for Iran.

title: GDP Value Added by 18 Economic-Activity Sectors, Current & Constant Prices (SH1390-1397)
notes: SCI's official national-accounts sector breakdown (agriculture/mining/manufacturing/services x18), both current and constant-1390-price bases -- finer sectoral detail than WDI's broad agriculture/industry/services shares. Contains a documented, preserved-not-resolved cross-table disagreement with gdp_by_expenditure_1390_1397.csv on 1393/1394 consumption-of-fixed-capital (both from the SAME source PDF).

title: GDP by Final Expenditure Component: Consumption, GFCF, Net Exports, Current & Constant Prices (SH1390-1397)
notes: SCI's official GDP-by-expenditure breakdown (private/government consumption, GFCF by machinery/construction x public/private, net exports, stock changes) -- companion table to gdp_value_added_by_sector_1390_1397.csv, same caveated cross-table disagreement on consumption-of-fixed-capital for 1393/1394.

title: Administrative Units (Shahrestan/Bakhsh/Shahr/Dehestan/Villages) by Province, 1996 Census
notes: OCR'd this round from a previously-flagged scanned-image PDF with zero text layer. 5 cells across 3 provinces left blank (genuine ink-blot scan damage, confirmed illegible via column-sum arithmetic cross-check against the printed national totals, not guessed).

title: Population by Sex and Urban/Rural-Resident/Non-Resident Status, 1996 Census
notes: Adds the sex x urban/rural/non-resident cross-tabulation for the 1996 census total (60,055,488), which the existing headline-totals-only chart (iran_census__national_census_population_totals_1956_1998, itself an extends-row on wdi__SP.POP) does not carry -- kept as its own new entry rather than a 2-hop extends chain.

title: Population by 8 Broad Age Groups incl. Sex Ratio, 1996 Census
notes: Age-band population x sex x sex-ratio for the 1996 census -- the 8-band totals sum to within 400 persons (0.0007%) of the printed national total, a minor internal source rounding artifact, preserved not corrected.

title: Population Share by Religion, National/Urban/Rural/Non-Resident, 1996 Census
notes: Adds a 15-year-earlier data point to the existing 2011-only religion-share chart. Deliberately incomplete: Christian/Jewish/Other rows only have the national-total column legible (urban/rural/non-resident cells illegible in this specific scan) -- see file's own caveats, do not treat missing cells as zero.

title: Literacy Rate by Age Group x Sex x Urban/Rural/Non-Resident, 1996 Census
notes: Adds a 10-year-earlier anchor point to the existing 2006-2016 literacy chart (iran_census__literacy_rate_2006_2016, itself an extends-row on wdi__SE.ADT.LITR), same underlying concept (literacy by age cohort and residence) from a decade before that chart's current start -- kept as its own new entry rather than a 2-hop extends chain.

title: School Enrollment (% in Education) by Age Group x Sex x Urban/Rural/Non-Resident, 1996 Census
notes: No existing Iran chart for school-enrollment-by-age-band at this granularity; complements WDI's aggregate gross/net enrollment ratios for Iran with a 1996 age x sex x residence cross-tabulation.

title: Population by Activity/Employment Status (Employed/Unemployed/Student/Homemaker/Other), 1996 Census
notes: Distinct concept from labor-force PARTICIPATION rate (iran_disasters__labor_force_participation_rate_urban_rural_2005_2014) -- this is the full activity-status distribution (including student/homemaker/other categories) for the whole population, not just the labor-force-eligible share. Hierarchical sub-categories flagged in notes (do not double-count when summing).

title: Maritime & Trade Facts Snapshot: Fleet, Ports, Seafarers (2024)
notes: First Iran-specific UNCTAD Maritime Country Profile extraction in this project (10 comparator-country profiles already downloaded but unextracted, noted as future work in the file's own README).

title: Merchandise Exports, Imports & Trade Balance (UNCTAD, 2005-2024 selected years)
notes: UNCTAD's own compiled merchandise trade series for Iran; sparse selected years as published in the Maritime Country Profile (not a project-imposed subsample). Likely conceptually overlaps WDI's own trade-value indicators for Iran -- kept as new pending a future cross-source dedup pass, since it arrives bundled with the maritime-specific tables in the same source document at effectively zero extra fetch cost.

title: Trade in Transport Services: Export Share by Category & Values (UNCTAD, 2005-2024 selected years)
notes: No existing Iran chart for transport-services trade breakdown (distinct from merchandise trade); 2024 column mostly blank in the source itself (not publishable), not an extraction gap.

title: National-Flag Merchant Fleet Carrying Capacity by Ship Type (UNCTAD/Clarksons, 2005-2024 selected years)
notes: Oil tankers, bulk carriers, general cargo, container ships by DWT -- no existing Iran fleet-composition chart in this project; directly relevant given Iran's sanctions-era reliance on its own tanker fleet for oil exports.

title: BGS World Mineral Statistics 1970-74 cross-check: Chrome Ore (Chromite)
notes: 220000/200000/140000/140000/140000 t (1970-74, all marked estimated). Independent BGS 1970-74 cross-check against USGS. GENUINE UNRESOLVED DISAGREEMENT with USGS on 1970/1974 values (BGS ~1.8x higher some years) -- investigated for a reconciling footnote this round, none found; both sources flag their own Iran chromite figures as estimates for every year, which explains but does not resolve the divergence. Both numbers preserved, not resolved to a winner.

title: BGS World Mineral Statistics 1970-74 cross-check: Lead (mine output, metal content)
notes: 22940/29937/30000/32000/42000 t (1970-74). Independent BGS cross-check -- closely agrees with USGS for all 5 years (1970 matches exactly: 22,940 t both sources), a reassuring cross-validation.

title: BGS World Mineral Statistics 1970-74 cross-check: Barium Minerals (Barite)
notes: 60219/60000/80000/80000/115000 t (1970-74). NEW 1970-74 data point (barite not present in the existing USGS-derived Iran mineral file's raw extraction) -- BGS is the only source for these specific years in this project.

title: BGS World Mineral Statistics 1970-74 cross-check: Coal (bituminous)
notes: 323000/400000/1000000/1000000/1200000 t (1970-74). NEW 1970-74 data point -- coal not present in the existing USGS-derived Iran mineral file's raw extraction. Shows a striking ~3.7x jump from 1971 (400,000t) to 1972 (1,000,000t).

title: BGS World Mineral Statistics 1970-74 cross-check: Gypsum
notes: 2,099,980/2,250,000/2,400,000/2,400,000/2,400,000 t (1970-74). Independent BGS cross-check against the existing 1968-1980 USGS-derived gypsum series -- see iran_mining_series/README.md for direct year-by-year comparison.

title: BGS World Mineral Statistics 1970-74 cross-check: Manganese Ore (gross weight, ~34% Mn)
notes: 9100/12300/9700/22000/30000 t (1970-74). NEW 1970-74 data point -- manganese not present in the existing USGS-derived Iran mineral file's raw extraction.

title: BGS World Mineral Statistics 1970-74 cross-check: Salt
notes: 350000/390000/400000/350000/400000 t (1970-74). NEW 1970-74 data point -- salt not present in the existing USGS-derived Iran mineral file's raw extraction.

title: BGS World Mineral Statistics 1970-74 cross-check: Sulphur (recovered/byproduct + mined ore, two sub-lines)
notes: Recovered: 412/495/666/595/~600 kt S; Ore: 1/2/3/3/(blank) kt S (1970-74). Independent BGS cross-check, conceptually comparable to (but not year-overlapping with) the existing USGS 'Sulfur total (native + byproduct of petroleum/gas)' 1976-80 rows -- see iran_mining_series/README.md caveats on the naming/methodology shift.

title: OPEC Facts & Figures Snapshot: Population, GDP, Trade, Energy (2024)
notes: 21-metric single-year cross-section from OPEC's flagship annual bulletin -- brings this project's Iran macro/energy snapshot up to the most current available year (2024) in one document.

title: OPEC-Reported Macro Aggregates: Population, GDP, Growth, Trade, Current Account (2020-2024)
notes: Likely conceptually overlaps WDI's own population/GDP/trade indicators for Iran -- kept as new (arrived at zero extra fetch cost bundled with the oil/gas tables from the same document) pending a future cross-source dedup pass; the OPEC-specific value here is the isolated petroleum-export-value line and internal consistency with the reserves/production tables in this same source.

title: Proven Crude Oil Reserves & Production (OPEC, 2020-2024)
notes: Reserves figure (208,600 million barrels, flat all 5 years) is likely genuinely new to this project; production figures extend/corroborate OWID's oil-production-by-country series with OPEC's own more current and authoritative numbers.

title: Crude Oil Production, Sparse Historical Anchors (1980/1990/2000/2010) Bridged to Present (2023/2024)
notes: The single most valuable OPEC find this round: a long-run bridge (1980-2024) for Iran's crude output in exactly the sparse-anchor format OPEC itself publishes, directly extending this project's Pahlavi-era oil series to the present day.

title: Natural Gas Reserves, Production, Exports, Imports & Demand (OPEC, 2020-2024)
notes: Full natural-gas balance for Iran (reserves flat at 33,988 bcm; marketed production, exports, imports near-zero, and domestic demand) -- no existing comparably complete Iran gas-balance chart in this project.

title: Iran Heavy Crude Oil Spot Price, OPEC Reference Basket Component (2020-2024 annual average)
notes: Iran's specific representative crude stream price within the ORB -- distinct from generic Brent/WTI benchmark series that may already exist elsewhere in this project.

title: GIEWS Wheat/Rice/Total-Cereal Production Estimates, as Reported Over Time (2010-2025)
notes: Independent FAO-GIEWS-analyst-assessed cereal production series (in-season forecasts and revisions, cited to satellite crop-monitoring + ministry announcements), complementing FAOSTAT's own official production numbers (data/processed/agriculture_qcl_production.csv, same FAO parent organization but a different, non-narrative statistical division) with a narrative/policy-context layer and higher-frequency in-season revisions not present in FAOSTAT's single annual figure.

title: GIEWS Cereal Import Requirement Forecasts by Marketing Year, incl. Maize/Wheat/Barley/Rice Split (2010/11-2026/27)
notes: No existing Iran chart for cereal-import forecasts by grain type; several years show a genuine large within-year forecast revision (flagged per-row) as the domestic harvest outlook became clearer mid-season -- do not average or pick one arbitrarily.

title: GIEWS-Reported Food-Price Inflation & Dual-Tier (Subsidized/Market) Exchange Rate (2012-2026)
notes: Independent secondary read on Iran's FX/inflation trajectory sourced from FAO GIEWS analysts (citing Iran's Central Bank), alongside the project's existing TGJU-sourced daily parallel-rate series -- the two are NOT identical concepts (GIEWS reports periodic point-in-time market-rate mentions plus food-specific inflation, not a continuous daily series), so treat as a corroborating secondary source rather than a duplicate; a future pass could formally cross-validate specific overlapping dates (e.g. this file's April-2026 market rate of IRR 1,317,000/USD against the daily series' own value for that date).

title: Government-Guaranteed Wheat Procurement Price, Common & Durum (2013-2026)
notes: Direct farm-gate policy-price series (distinct from retail/market prices) -- shows the price trajectory from IRR 8,000/kg (2013) to the equivalent of IRR 496,500/kg (2026, +140% YoY alone), a striking data point for Iran's agricultural-subsidy and currency-depreciation story. One toman-to-rial unit conversion performed and flagged (see file's own notes).
